title: Effects of Al contents on aging hardening behaviors of Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels with ultra-high specific hardness
abstract_summary: Fe-35Mn-(15,17,19)Al-5Cu (at.%) low-density steels were developed to achieve a wide hardness range (253.4–965.6 HV) via simple aging, driven by continuous β-Mn precipitation. Increasing Al content slowed β-Mn precipitation kinetics, slightly raised β-Mn intrinsic hardness, but reduced the FCC Cu-rich phase at β-Mn interfaces and decreased fracture toughness, thereby diminishing the final hardening effect. The Fe-35.2Mn-15Al-5.2Cu alloy reached an ultra-high specific hardness of 141 HV·cm³/g.
research_objective: To systematically investigate the influence of Al content on the aging hardening behavior, β-Mn precipitation kinetics, and resulting hardness/toughness trade-off in Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels.
material_systems: Primary phases: β-Mn, FCC Cu-rich phase, α (parent phase)
main_process_variables: Cooling methods: oil quench (solution), water quench (aging)

key_sections.role: Background on low-density steels, challenge of high hardness, role of β-Mn and Al, objectives of current work
Alloy preparation, heat treatment schedules, hardness measurement and microstructure characterization techniques
Aging hardening curves, phase evolution (XRD, SEM, EBSD), TEM observations of β-Mn and Cu-rich phase, intrinsic hardness of β-Mn
Effect of Al on aging hardening, precipitation kinetics, fracture toughness, and insights into specific hardness advantages over other structural alloys
Summary of Al effects on wide-range hardness, precipitation kinetics, β-Mn hardening, and specific hardness
